name: I fell off a ladder carrying heavy stuff at work, whose fault is it?
description: Employer/GC for safety failures; comp no-fault.[1][3]

mainEntity.name: I fell off a ladder carrying heavy stuff at work, whose fault is it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Employer/GC for safety failures; comp no-fault.[1][3]
acceptedAnswer.description:
Your employer or general contractor may be liable for not providing help or proper safety under labor laws.[3]

Ladders must be suitable; failure to assist with loads shifts responsibility.[1] Workers' comp is no-fault, but suits target negligence.

- Owner/contractor for fall protection.
- Supervisor for oversight.
- Manufacturer if defective.

Investigate via lawyer; NY Scaffold Law exemplifies strict liability.[2] Fault impacts extra compensation.
